title: Faith, Testimony, and Assurance
subtitle: Victory through faith, the threefold witness, confidence in eternal life

heb.gloss: to beget, bear, give birth to
heb.paragraph: Everyone who believes that Jesus is the Christ has been born of God - faith is evidence of divine birth, not its cause.
This is the victory that has overcome the world - our faith - faith is the instrument of victory over the world system.
Who is it that overcomes the world except the one who believes Jesus is the Son of God - faith in Jesus identity is world-conquering power.

panels.ctx: Everyone who believes that Jesus is the Christ has been born of God, and everyone who loves the Father loves whoever has been born of him. By this we know that we love the children of God, when we love God and obey his commandments. For this is the love of God, that we keep his commandments. And his commandments are not burdensome. For everyone who has been born of God overcomes the world. And this is the victory that has overcome the world - our faith. Who is it that overcomes the world except the one who believes that Jesus is the Son of God?

cross.note: In the world you will have tribulation. But take heart; I have overcome the world.
My yoke is easy, and my burden is light.
To the one who conquers I will grant to eat of the tree of life.
In all these things we are more than conquerors through him who loved us.

notes.note: Everyone believing Jesus is the Christ has been born of God. Everyone who loves the Father loves whoever has been born of him.
Love for God means keeping his commandments - and they are not burdensome. Everyone born of God overcomes the world. This is the victory: our faith.
Calvin notes the logical chain: faith evidences new birth; new birth produces love for God siblings; love for siblings proves love for Father.
Faith overcomes by connecting us to the One who has already overcome. Our victory is participation in his victory.

netbible.source: NET Bible Full Notes Edition — Biblical Studies Press

notes.note: Has been born (gegenneetai) is perfect tense - prior completed action. Faith reveals what God has already done.
Has overcome (nikesasa) is aorist - victory viewed as accomplished fact. Believers share Christ decisive victory.

yarbrough.source: Robert Yarbrough, BECNT 1-3 John — Scholarly Paraphrase

notes.note: Yarbrough emphasizes that faith is not self-generated power but connection to Christ power. We overcome by believing in the Overcomer.

kruse.source: Colin Kruse, TNTC/Pillar 1-3 John — Scholarly Paraphrase

notes.note: Kruse notes that commandments are not burdensome because the Spirit enables obedience. Love transforms duty into delight.

panels.hist: First John addresses a crisis caused by secessionists who have left the Johannine community (2:19). These opponents likely held proto-Gnostic views: denying that Jesus Christ came 'in the flesh' (4:2), claiming sinless perfection while living immorally (1:8-10), and failing to love fellow believers (2:9-11). The author, traditionally identified as the apostle John writing from Ephesus in the 90s AD, establishes tests of authentic Christianity: right belief about Christ's incarnation, righteous living, and love for the community. The letter's repetitive, spiral structure reinforces these themes through variation rather than linear argument. The community had experienced a traumatic split, and the letter reassures those who remained of their standing with God.

heb.paragraph: This is he who came by water and blood - water refers to Jesus baptism, blood to his death. Both are essential.
There are three that testify: the Spirit, the water, and the blood - the threefold witness confirms Christ identity.
God gave us eternal life, and this life is in his Son - eternal life is not merely duration but quality of relationship.

panels.ctx: This is he who came by water and blood - Jesus Christ; not by the water only but by the water and the blood. And the Spirit is the one who testifies, because the Spirit is the truth. For there are three that testify: the Spirit and the water and the blood; and these three agree. If we receive the testimony of men, the testimony of God is greater, for this is the testimony of God that he has borne concerning his Son. Whoever believes in the Son of God has the testimony in himself. Whoever does not believe God has made him a liar, because he has not believed in the testimony that God has borne concerning his Son. And this is the testimony, that God gave us eternal life, and this life is in his Son. Whoever has the Son has life; whoever does not have the Son of God does not have life.

cross.note: One of the soldiers pierced his side, and at once there came out blood and water.
Jesus was baptized and the Spirit descended - the baptism testimony.
When the Helper comes, he will bear witness about me.
Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life.

notes.note: Jesus came by water and blood - not water only but water and blood. The Spirit testifies because the Spirit is truth. Three witnesses agree.
God testimony is greater than human testimony. Whoever believes has the testimony in himself. God gave us eternal life; this life is in his Son.
Calvin interprets water and blood as baptism and death - both are necessary. The false teachers may have accepted baptism but denied atoning death.
Life is in the Son, not apart from him. Having the Son means having life; not having the Son means not having life. No middle ground.

netbible.source: NET Bible Full Notes Edition — Biblical Studies Press

notes.note: Water and blood probably refer to Jesus baptism and death. Some false teachers may have separated the heavenly Christ from the earthly Jesus.
The longer reading (Comma Johanneum) adding heavenly witnesses is not original. Early manuscripts lack it; it entered later.

yarbrough.source: Robert Yarbrough, BECNT 1-3 John — Scholarly Paraphrase

notes.note: Yarbrough emphasizes the christological focus: the whole passage concerns the identity and work of Jesus. Eternal life is knowing him.

kruse.source: Colin Kruse, TNTC/Pillar 1-3 John — Scholarly Paraphrase

notes.note: Kruse notes that the three witnesses converge on one truth: Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God. Multiple testimony confirms the gospel.

heb.paragraph: I write these things that you may know that you have eternal life - assurance is John purpose. Knowledge is possible.
This is the confidence we have toward him - boldness in prayer flows from relationship. We ask according to his will.
Little children, keep yourselves from idols - the abrupt closing warns against any substitute for the true God.

panels.ctx: I write these things to you who believe in the name of the Son of God, that you may know that you have eternal life. And this is the confidence that we have toward him, that if we ask anything according to his will he hears us. And if we know that he hears us in whatever we ask, we know that we have the requests that we have asked of him. If anyone sees his brother committing a sin not leading to death, he shall ask, and God will give him life - to those who commit sins that do not lead to death. There is sin that leads to death; I do not say that one should pray for that. All wrongdoing is sin, but there is sin that does not lead to death. We know that everyone who has been born of God does not keep on sinning, but he who was born of God protects him, and the evil one does not touch him. We know that we are from God, and the whole world lies in the power of the evil one. And we know that the Son of God has come and has given us understanding, so that we may know him who is true; and we are in him who is true, in his Son Jesus Christ. He is the true God and eternal life. Little children, keep yourselves from idols.

cross.note: These are written so that you may believe and have life in his name.
Whatever you ask in my name, this I will do.
Whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven - unforgivable sin.

notes.note: I write so that you who believe may know you have eternal life - assurance is the purpose.
Confidence in prayer according to his will. Pray for a brother sinning - God will give life. But there is sin leading to death.
Everyone born of God does not keep on sinning. The Son of God has come and given us understanding. Keep yourselves from idols.
Calvin emphasizes that assurance is God intended gift. Doubt is not humility but failure to receive what God offers.
Sin leading to death is most likely apostasy - deliberate, final rejection of Christ. For such, prayer is not prescribed.
The closing warning seems abrupt but fits the context: anything that takes the place of the true God revealed in Christ is idolatry.

netbible.source: NET Bible Full Notes Edition — Biblical Studies Press

notes.note: Sin leading to death is debated: apostasy, blasphemy against the Spirit, or sin resulting in physical death. The first fits the context best.
He is the true God - the antecedent could be God the Father or Jesus Christ. Grammar slightly favors Christ, which would be a clear deity claim.

yarbrough.source: Robert Yarbrough, BECNT 1-3 John — Scholarly Paraphrase

notes.note: Yarbrough notes the threefold we know (vv. 18, 19, 20) - John wants certainty, not doubt. Knowledge is both possible and intended.

kruse.source: Colin Kruse, TNTC/Pillar 1-3 John — Scholarly Paraphrase

notes.note: Kruse emphasizes that the closing warnings balance assurance. Confidence is not complacency; vigilance against idols is still required.

themes.note: Faith dominates as the means of victory and assurance. Sovereignty appears in the Son protection and the evil one limitation. The true God is the object of faith and source of life.

rows.label: vv. 6-12
rows.text: The three witnesses; life in the Son

lit.note: Chapter 5 concludes with assurance: faith overcomes, witnesses confirm, and believers can know they have eternal life. The abrupt closing warns against idols.
